This fresh isolated-target run **supersedes 156.591s** as the current clean-release figure (the ~8.8s delta is run-to-run variance; both confirm MISS, ≈23% over budget); older rows kept as history. **Current state / root cause:** the clean build is bounded by *one serial unit*, not by core count — `cranelift-codegen` 0.131.1 is a single **125.64s** rustc unit (85% of the 147.66s critical wall), on the critical path 22.02s→build-end while concurrency was already 16/16 cores. It is pulled by `wasmtime` 44.0.1; on the critical-path attribution that `cargo tree -i wasmtime` surfaces, wasmtime is reached via `rge-expr-wasm` + `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine` (the scripting/plugin WASM runtime) — but that is the attribution path, not the full direct-dependency set. A direct manifest scan shows **four** workspace crates declare `wasmtime` directly: `rge-expr-wasm`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, and `rge-script-bench`; the wasm/wasmtime/cranelift family totals 340.8s CPU over 54 units. Estimated post-fix floor ~110s (next tail = `rge-editor` / `rge-tool-architecture-lints` bin links), i.e. cutting the Cranelift pole alone would likely cross under budget. **Next handoff options (none implemented; file as a separate remediation task):** (A) `[profile.release.package."cranelift-codegen"]` lower `opt-level` — large upside / low–medium risk / automatable only after a perf re-check; (B) wasmtime Pulley interpreter (`default-features=false`, drop cranelift/winch) applied across **all four** direct dependents (`rge-expr-wasm`, `rge-runtime-wasmtime-engine`, `rge-script-host`, `rge-script-bench` — scoping to only the first two leaves script-host/script-bench still pulling cranelift/winch) — largest upside but high runtime-behavior risk / not automatable; (C) feature-gate the wasm scripting stack (all four direct dependents) out of the default build — large upside / high build-surface risk / not automatable. Proof-of-improvement later = re-run the same `--timings` isolated build and confirm Total ≤120s and Cranelift no longer the tail. Ranked tables in `plans/BASELINE.md` §13.3. **Not changed:** no source, tests, Cargo, tooling, workflow, scheduler, or dispatch automation behavior; artifacts under gitignored `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-322/`; scratch target verified under prefix and removed (2.28 GB freed).

+# Exec Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-329
+AUTHOR: Executor / Codex salvage after guard abort
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-06-08_05-14-06+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- plans/BASELINE.md
+- Status.md
+- HANDOFF.md
+- change.md
+- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md
+- Cargo.toml
+STATUS: COMPLETE
+
+## Summary
+
+Task 90 candidate A was measured and rejected. The temporary root
+`Cargo.toml` `[profile.release.package."cranelift-codegen"] opt-level = 1`
+override was tested from fresh isolated target
+`B:\sdk\rge-cranelift-opt1-ISSUE-329-20260608-045939` with
+`cargo build --workspace --release --timings`.
+
+The build exited 0 but regressed clean-release time:
+
+- Cargo `Finished`: **2m 58s**
+- Cargo `--timings` Total: **178.4s (2m 58.4s)**
+- `cranelift-codegen`: **148.38s**, start 30.01s, end 178.39s
+- Critical-path verdict: `cranelift-codegen` remained the build tail
+
+This is worse than the ISSUE-322 reference of 147.82s total with
+`cranelift-codegen` at 125.64s, and it still misses the <=120s clean-build
+budget. The Cargo profile override was reverted and is not retained.
+
+## Notes
+
+The focused release `rge-script-bench` Cranelift check was started, but did not
+complete because `Invoke-AiDispatchGuard.ps1` aborted the run on a monitor JSON
+parse error after the failed build measurement. Because the clean-build gate had
+already failed decisively, no Cargo profile change was kept.
+
+## Verification
+
+- `cargo build --workspace --release --timings` from the fresh isolated target
+  exited 0.
+- The Cargo timing HTML was parsed locally to confirm total time and the
+  `cranelift-codegen` critical-path tail.
+- `git diff --check` is clean for the final docs-only diff.
+
+## Non-Goals
+
+No Rust source/tests, dependency features, `Cargo.lock`, workflows, scheduler
+config, dispatch automation scripts, architecture lints, committed timing
+artifacts, `cargo clean`, or shared `A:\RustCache\target` deletion.

+## Goal
+
+Execute GitHub issue #329 / dispatch task 90: test the smallest release-profile remediation candidate from the ISSUE-322 clean-release hotspot attribution by temporarily adding a root `Cargo.toml` package override for `cranelift-codegen` with `opt-level = 1`, measuring a true clean release workspace build from a fresh isolated `B:\sdk` target with Cargo `--timings`, and keeping the override only if it materially improves the clean-build result without tripping the focused Wasmtime/Cranelift script-bench assertions.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `Cargo.toml` only to add the minimal `[profile.release.package."cranelift-codegen"]` / `opt-level = 1` override, and only keep that edit if the acceptance criteria below justify it.
+- `plans/BASELINE.md` only to record the experiment result, wall time, Cargo "Finished" time, Cargo timings total, critical-path-tail finding, and keep/revert verdict.
+- `Status.md` only to prepend the dated ISSUE-329 status snapshot.
+- `HANDOFF.md` only to prepend the dated ISSUE-329 handoff snapshot.
+- `change.md` only to append one chronological ISSUE-329 entry.
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` only to mark task 90 done or done-blocked after the result is recorded.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `**/*.rs`
+- `crates/**/Cargo.toml`
+- `editor/**/Cargo.toml`
+- `kernel/**/Cargo.toml`
+- `runtime/**/Cargo.toml`
+- `tools/**/Cargo.toml`
+- `.github/workflows/**`
+- `tools/architecture-lints/**`

+- `ai_handoffs/*_CLOSEOUT_*.md`
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-329_EXEC_*.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-329_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- `**/*.rs`
+- `Cargo.lock`

+
+### Advisory machine-readable scope envelope
+
+<!-- handoff:envelope v1 -->
+MAY_EDIT:

+INCIDENTAL_OK: false
+<!-- /handoff:envelope -->
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- A minimal root `Cargo.toml` experiment that adds only:
+
+  ```toml
+  [profile.release.package."cranelift-codegen"]
+  opt-level = 1
+  ```
+
+- A true clean release build measurement using a fresh isolated target directory under `B:\sdk`, with no `cargo clean` and no use or deletion of `A:\RustCache\target`.
+- A focused release-mode Wasmtime/Cranelift check using `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_cranelift::tests -- --nocapture`.
+- Forward-only records in `plans/BASELINE.md`, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `change.md` that include wall time, Cargo "Finished" time, Cargo timings total, whether `cranelift-codegen` remains the critical-path tail, the focused test result, and the final keep/revert decision.
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task 90 marked done if the override is kept, or done-blocked / failed-experiment if the override is reverted after measurement.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The clean build is run from an empty target directory that resolves under `B:\sdk`, preferably via `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=B:\sdk\rge-cranelift-opt1-ISSUE-329-<timestamp>`, and the command executed is `cargo build --workspace --release --timings`.
+- The shared `A:\RustCache\target` is not cleaned, deleted, repointed as the active target, or otherwise modified by this dispatch.
+- `Cargo.toml` is kept only if the measured clean release result materially improves against the ISSUE-322 current clean-release reference of 147.82 s wall / Cargo 2m 27s / timings critical wall 147.66 s, satisfies the PLAN §13.3 clean-build budget of <= 120 s, and the focused Wasmtime/Cranelift check exits 0 within its assertions.
+- If the clean build still misses the <= 120 s budget, `cranelift-codegen` remains the critical-path tail, timings cannot establish a material improvement, or the focused check fails under the override, revert the `Cargo.toml` change and land only the documentation/status/task-record failed-experiment result.
+- Final tracked diffs are limited to the MAY edit / MAY add surface above; no committed throw-away timing artifacts, target files, logs, or Cargo timing HTML files remain in the repo.
+
+## Current-State Claims: Falsification Required
+
+- Claim: the root manifest does not already contain a `[profile.release.package."cranelift-codegen"]` override before this dispatch.
+  Falsifying search: `Select-String -LiteralPath 'Cargo.toml' -Pattern '[profile.release.package."cranelift-codegen"]' -SimpleMatch` -> no matches.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not edit Rust source, Rust tests, dependency features, `Cargo.lock`, workflows, scheduler configuration, dispatch automation scripts, architecture lints, or committed throw-away timing artifacts.
+- Do not run `cargo clean`.
+- Do not touch shared `A:\RustCache\target`; use a fresh isolated `B:\sdk` target for the clean build and avoid shared-target side effects for the focused test.
+- Do not change global `[profile.release]` settings; the only allowed profile experiment is the package-specific `cranelift-codegen` `opt-level = 1` override.
+- Do not change Wasmtime features, switch runtimes, remove dependencies, add scripts, add harnesses, or broaden the experiment beyond candidate A.
+- Preserve dated history: prepend `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` snapshots, append one `change.md` entry, and add forward-only baseline text instead of rewriting prior historical measurements.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each required gate in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- With `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` set to a fresh empty directory under `B:\sdk`: `cargo build --workspace --release --timings` -> expected exit 0 for a valid measurement.
+- With `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` set to the same or another isolated `B:\sdk` target, not `A:\RustCache\target`: `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_cranelift::tests -- --nocapture` -> expected exit 0 if the override is eligible to keep.
+- `git diff --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` -> run only if formatting-relevant changes are made; expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` -> must show only allowed tracked paths before handoff.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- `B:\sdk` is unavailable or a fresh isolated target under `B:\sdk` cannot be created without touching `A:\RustCache\target`.
+- The work appears to require any Rust source/test edit, dependency-feature change, `Cargo.lock` update, workflow edit, scheduler/automation edit, architecture-lint edit, or new committed timing artifact.
+- Cargo attempts to update `Cargo.lock`, or the build cannot proceed without accepting a lockfile change.
+- The Cargo timings output cannot be located or parsed enough to report timings total and whether `cranelift-codegen` remains the critical-path tail.
+- Any tracked diff appears outside the MAY edit / MAY add surface and is not plainly an unrelated pre-existing user change.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The relevant prior baseline is `plans/BASELINE.md` §13.3: ISSUE-322 recorded a true clean release `cargo build --workspace --release --timings` at 147.82 s wall, Cargo 2m 27s, timings critical wall 147.66 s, with `cranelift-codegen` 0.131.1 as the single critical-path tail at 125.64 s. Candidate A is intentionally the least invasive remediation: a package-specific release opt-level override. This dispatch should produce either a kept one-block `Cargo.toml` improvement or a docs-only failed-experiment record; it must not drift into Wasmtime feature surgery or source/harness work.
+
